items.title: Bitcoin's quantum debate splits as Adam Back pushes optional upgrades over forced freeze
items.summary: The Blockstream CEO told Paris Blockchain Week that Bitcoin should build quantum-resistant upgrades now, a day after Jameson Lopp proposed freezing vulnerable coins instead.

items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Try our new dimensional analysis Claude plugin
items.summary: We’re releasing a new Claude plugin for developing and auditing code that implements dimensional analysis, a technique we explored in our most recent blog post. Most LLM-based security skills ask the model to find bugs. Our new dimensional-analysis plugin for Claude Code takes a

items.title: Spotting issues in DeFi with dimensional analysis
items.summary: Using dimensional analysis, you can categorically rule out a whole category of logic and arithmetic bugs that plague DeFi formulas. No code changes required, just better reasoning! One of the first lessons in physics is learning to think in terms of dimensions. Physicists can oft

items.title: Bitcoin bulls target $125,000 as U.S.-Iran peace talks trigger risk-on mood
items.summary: Funding rates at 2023 lows signal the market is heavily short against bitcoin, ZeroStack's Daniel Reis-Faria says, setting up conditions for a forced unwind if prices push higher.

items.title: Crypto for Advisors: Tokenization’s evolution
items.summary: Tokenized assets are moving from concept to portfolio allocation. Learn how compliance architecture and institutional movement are redefining risk and opportunity for advisors.

items.title: South Korea to test blockchain deposit tokens for government spending in Q4
items.summary: Token-based payments can be programmed with spending limits and which industries can use them, reducing audits and lowering transaction fees by removing intermediaries.

items.title: Ethereum just had its busiest quarter ever, capping a three-year comeback
items.summary: Quarterly transactions hit 200.4 million in Q1 2026, the first time above 200 million and more than double the 2023 lows.

items.title: This simple indicator has called every bottom since 2015. It hasn't triggered yet
items.summary: A bitcoin indicator based on key moving averages has accurately marked every bear market bottom since 2015. That signal hasn't fired yet.

items.title: The U.S. government moves $606,000 in bitcoin linked to the 2016 Bitfinex hack to Coinbase
items.summary: Bitfinex plans to use the returned coins to redeem all Recovery Right Tokens and devote at least 80 percent of remaining net proceeds to repurchasing and burning its UNUS SED LEO token.

items.title: XRP leads bitcoin and ether on weekly gains, but muted volume keeps breakout in check
items.summary: Token outperforms bitcoin and ether over seven days, though thinning participation signals consolidation rather than conviction.

items.title: Texas man in $20M Meta-1 Coin fraud sentenced to 23 years in prison
items.summary: Meta-1 Coin was sold and marketed from 2018 to 2023 as an investment backed by $44 billion in gold and $1 billion in artworks, which turned out to be fictional.

items.title: Crypto in sustained winter as CEX volumes drop 39% in Q1: CoinGecko
items.summary: March was the “weakest month" with $800 billion in centralized crypto exchange trading volume, the lowest since November 2023.

items.title: Cybersecurity researcher uncovers fake Ledger sold on Chinese marketplace
items.summary: The researcher said they examined the fake Ledger device’s firmware and found signs pointing to a Chinese semiconductor company named Espressif Systems.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation-funded program exposes 100 DPRK workers in crypto
items.summary: The Ketman Project, funded by an Ethereum Foundation stipend, identified 100 North Korean IT workers and alerted about 53 projects employing DPRK operatives.

items.title: At least a dozen crypto entities attacked since Drift Protocol hack
items.summary: Rhea Finance and the Russia-linked Grinex exchange were hacked for a combined $21 million over the past two days.

items.title: Zanzibar police probe crypto exec Joe McCann after fiancée’s death
items.summary: Zanzibar police are reportedly holding Asymmetric founder Joe McCann for questioning after the death of his fiancée, Ashly Robinson.

items.title: Russia-linked crypto exchange Grinex halts trading after $14M hack
items.summary: Grinex has been accused by US authorities of assisting Russia and other entities in evading sanctions and laundering funds for Russia-linked hackers.

items.title: Ex-Treasury chief warns of US bond crash, calls for contingency plan
items.summary: “When we hit it, it will be vicious, so we have to prepare for that eventuality,” said former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson on a potential US Treasury market crisis.

items.title: Three things Bitcoin must do to hold highs above $76K: Analysts
items.summary: Analysts say reclaiming $76,000, sustained spot market buy volume and consistent inflows to the Bitcoin ETFs are the necessary components required for a rally to new highs.
